title: Smoke on the Water (Main Riff)
description: The main riff. Four-note motif played in fourths on the D and G strings. The rhythm — not the notes — is what trips people up.
practice: Play the riff at 60 BPM with a metronome on every beat.
Once clean, drop the metronome to beats 2 and 4 only — find the pocket.
Sing or hum the rhythm before playing it. The rhythm should be in your body before your hands.
Record yourself and listen back for timing slips on the syncopated entry.

resources.title: Smoke on the Water — slow-motion riff breakdown
Smoke on the Water (main riff TAB)

title: House of the Rising Sun (Intro Arpeggio)
description: The iconic Am–C–D–F–Am arpeggio pattern in 6/8. Every note rings individually in a flowing triplet feel. This piece teaches open chord transitions, fingerpicking coordination, and the 6/8 feel simultaneously.
keyTechniques: Open chord transitions (Am, C, D, F)
practice: Learn the chord progression: Am–C–D–F–Am. Transition without rushing.
Play the arpeggio pattern slowly with a pick first, then practice fingerpicking.
Use a metronome set to the dotted-quarter pulse (two beats per bar in 6/8).
Record yourself and check that every note in each arpeggio rings fully.

resources.title: House of the Rising Sun — fingerpicking TAB

title: Come As You Are (Intro Riff)
description: Kurt Cobain's signature intro riff — a single-note melody using a compressed, slightly chorused clean tone. Simple note choices, but the feel and subtle expression require real control. A great first lesson in single-note melodic playing.
practice: Learn the riff slowly with strict alternate picking — every note, no exceptions.
Once the notes are clean, add the slight bend at the end of each phrase.
Play with a clean tone and listen for evenness of attack across all the notes.
Record at 100% speed and check for any note that pops out louder than the others.

resources.title: Come As You Are — intro riff TAB
